instructions.name: attach_node
docs: Task 10 — `attach_node`: set the wallet↔node POSSESSION link
(`Vault.node`). The USER's vault passkey MUST sign a domain-bound
op-message (`b"attach_node" || vault || node`), verified via the
SIMD-0075 precompile sibling. Link-once: re-link is rejected.

accounts.name: vault
docs: The USER's vault — receives the `node` link. Mutated, no signer required:
the user's passkey signature (verified via the SIMD-0075 precompile
sibling) is what authorizes the mutation.

accounts.name: node
docs: The node this vault is being linked to. Read-only here — `attach_node`
only writes the pointer onto the vault. Must already exist (a
`PrincipalNode` created by `create_node`).

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: instruction in the transaction MUST be a secp256r1_verify call carrying
the USER's passkey signature over the op-message.

instructions.name: attach_root
docs: Task 10 — `attach_root`: point an EXISTING node UP at an existing human
`CreditRoot` (wallet-first acquire-a-root). The node's controller signs;
`node.parent = None` and `root_attestation = Some(credit_root)`. Multiple
nodes on the SAME root aggregate under it (one-human-one-root by
aggregation). Non-transferable: never cleared once set.

accounts.name: node
docs: The node being rooted in place. Mutated (`parent` + `root_attestation`).

accounts.name: node_controller
docs: The node's controller — must sign (possession of the node). Bound to
`node.controller` so only the controller may root this node.

accounts.name: credit_root
docs: The human `CreditRoot`. MUST already exist — `Account<CreditRoot>` with
seeds `[CREDIT_ROOT_SEED, nullifier]` + the stored bump deserializes an
existing account; a missing one fails to load. NOT init: this instruction
only POINTS at an existing root, never creates one.

accounts.name: vault
docs: `has_one` enforces vault.dexter_authority == dexter_authority.key()
(same authority gate as close_session / settle_locked_voucher).

accounts.name: claim
docs: The TERMINAL claim being reclaimed. `close = dexter_authority` drains all
lamports to the authority, reassigns to System, resizes to 0 — no revival
(same semantics close_session relies on). Bound to this vault.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: The vault's recorded authority — signs and receives the reclaimed rent
(the party that operationally fronted it). Mirrors close_session.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Read-only: the version + authority gates read it, nothing mutates it.
`has_one` enforces vault.dexter_authority == dexter_authority.key()
(the same authority-gate shape settle_voucher uses, same error mapping).

accounts.name: session
docs: The CLEARED session PDA being closed. A cleared session has every
SessionRegistration field zeroed (including `allowed_counterparty`), so
the binding to (vault, counterparty) cannot be read from the account
body — the PDA SEEDS are the proof: only the canonical PDA for
(vault, args.allowed_counterparty) satisfies this constraint. Bump from
stored (`session.bump`), matching revoke_session_key — register stamps
the canonical bump at first touch and neither revoke nor the register-
time expired-sibling sweep ever zeroes `bump` (or `vault`), so it is
valid on a cleared account.
`close = dexter_authority` — Anchor 0.32.1 close semantics (verified in
anchor-lang 0.32.1 src/common.rs::close): drains ALL lamports to the
destination, reassigns the account to the System Program, and resizes
data to 0. No stale bytes survive and the account is no longer
program-owned, so a same-transaction revival (re-funding the emptied
account with stale state) is structurally impossible — there is no
state left to revive and `Account<SessionAccount>` deserialization
would fail the owner check anyway.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: The vault's recorded dexter_authority — must sign, and receives the
reclaimed rent (hence `mut`). Authority-only in v1 by design: the
authority operationally funds session-PDA rent in the hosted flow, so
the rent returns to the party that fronted it.

instructions.name: close_standby
docs: Task 5 — `close_standby` → CUT-EDGE: prune a child edge. The immediate
parent's controller signs (SHRINK); the child must have ZERO outstanding
draw (`borrowed == 0 && subtree_draw == 0`) — the anti-liability-shed gate
the recourse cascade depends on. Sets `node.parent = None`, decrements
`parent.child_count`.

accounts.name: node
docs: The CHILD node being cut from its parent. Mutated: `parent` → None.

accounts.name: parent_node
docs: The immediate PARENT. Mutated: `child_count` and `subtree_draw` decrement.

accounts.name: parent_controller
docs: The parent's controller — SHRINK consent. Address-constrained to
`parent_node.controller` and required to SIGN: only the parent that took
recourse for this child may release it.

instructions.name: create_node
docs: Task 3 — create a `PrincipalNode`: an anonymous root-less node
(`args.parent == None`) or a delegate (`Some` ⇒ parent's controller must
sign = GROW consent, child cap fits under the parent). `init` so each
node_id can be created at most once.

accounts.name: node
docs: The new node. `init` (NOT init_if_needed): existence per node_id is the
uniqueness guard.

accounts.name: controller
docs: The new node's controller — must sign (it is consenting to own this node).

accounts.name: payer
docs: Rent payer for the new node account.

accounts.name: parent_node
docs: The parent node, present ONLY for a delegate (args.parent == Some). Its
`controller` must sign via `parent_controller` (GROW consent, S1 D3).

accounts.name: parent_controller
docs: The parent node's controller — GROW consent. Present (and signing) ONLY
for a delegate. The address constraint binds it to the supplied
`parent_node.controller`, so an attacker cannot pass an arbitrary signer.
Omitting it on a delegate create makes the tx unsigned for the parent →
hard reject (the handler requires it Present on the delegate path).
When BOTH are None (root-less create) the constraint is a no-op.
NOTE: within an `Option<Signer>` account constraint, Anchor evaluates the
expression ONLY when the account is present, and `parent_controller` here
is the inner `AccountInfo` (not the Option). So this binds a PRESENT
parent_controller to the parent's controller; absence is handled in the
handler (delegate path requires it Present).

accounts.name: graph_config
docs: The graph admin spine — read for the global pause flag.

accounts.name: financier_swig
docs: Position 0 — the FINANCIER's swig (required at this index by swig's
ProgramExec validator). The money for the draw comes from here.

accounts.name: financier_swig_wallet_address
docs: Position 1 — the financier's swig_wallet_address PDA. The following SignV2
spends THIS ATA's owner.

accounts.name: vault
docs: The USER's vault. Bound to the drawing node via `vault.node`.

accounts.name: drawing_node
docs: The PrincipalNode whose `RateCap` authorizes (and whose `borrowed`
accumulates) this draw. Must equal `vault.node`. Depth-1: this node must
itself be rooted (`root_attestation.is_some()`); the N-level authenticated
ancestor traverse lands in Task 6.

accounts.name: graph_config
docs: Graph admin spine — read for the global pause gate.

accounts.name: seller_destination
docs: The booked seller destination ATA. The following SignV2 TransferChecked's
destination MUST equal this key, else `DebtTransferMismatch`. Bound by key
only; this instruction never reads its data.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: The orchestration authority driving the draw. Matches settle_locked_voucher.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: instruction is the swig::SignV2 this handler introspects for the money leg.

instructions.name: emancipate
docs: Task 9 — `emancipate`: a node cuts its parent edge and re-roots itself.
`close_standby` (cut-edge) + `attach_root` (acquire-a-root) fused: both
controllers sign (parent RELEASES, node ACCEPTS); gated on zero outstanding
(`subtree_draw == 0 && borrowed == 0` — the anti-liability-shed invariant
the cascade depends on). `new_nullifier == Some` ⇒ acquire the human
`CreditRoot` at `[CREDIT_ROOT_SEED, nullifier]`; `None` ⇒ keep any existing
attestation. Sets `node.parent = None`, decrements `parent.child_count`.
Non-transferable: only ever sets `root_attestation` from None.

accounts.name: node
docs: The node emancipating. Mutated: `parent` → None, and (Some path)
`root_attestation` → the acquired credit root.

accounts.name: parent_node
docs: The immediate PARENT being cut. Mutated: `child_count` and `subtree_draw`
decrement. Must be the node's current parent (the edge we're cutting).

accounts.name: parent_controller
docs: The parent's controller — SHRINK RELEASE consent. Bound to
`parent_node.controller` and required to SIGN.

accounts.name: node_controller
docs: The node's own controller — ACCEPT consent. Bound to `node.controller` and
required to SIGN (the node accepts being emancipated / re-rooted).

accounts.name: credit_root
docs: The human `CreditRoot` to acquire. Present (and bound in the handler to
`[CREDIT_ROOT_SEED, new_nullifier]`) ONLY on the `new_nullifier == Some`
path. `Account<CreditRoot>` already enforces owner + discriminator; the
handler additionally binds it to the declared nullifier.

accounts.name: graph_config
docs: The graph admin spine. De-risking verbs (cut-edge, repay, seize,
emancipate) stay LIVE through pause/freeze — exactly when an operator most
needs to unwind exposure — so this is read for context, NOT a pause gate.

accounts.name: root_cache
docs: The singleton World ID root cache. Read-only here; its `root` is the
value the proof's merkle_root MUST match (`bind_world_id_context`).

accounts.name: credit_root
docs: The one-human-one-root PDA. `init` (NOT init_if_needed): the PDA's
existence IS the uniqueness guard — a second proof for the same human
yields the same nullifier seed, so this `init` fails ("already in use").
The seed uses `args.public_inputs[0]` directly; the handler verifies the
proof and re-derives the same value via `bind_world_id_context`, so the
PDA seed and the stored nullifier are provably the same verified value.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays rent for the new CreditRoot. Anyone may pay; the human's binding is
in the proof, not in who funds the PDA.

instructions.name: establish_credit_root_trusted
docs: Trusted (off-chain-verified) personhood → CreditRoot. Authority-gated
sibling of `establish_credit_root`: writes the same CreditRoot PDA from
an operator-attested nullifier (e.g. a v3 Orb proof verified off-chain),
no on-chain groth16. `version = 2` marks the trusted attestation.

accounts.name: credit_root
docs: The one-human-one-root PDA. `init` (NOT init_if_needed): existence IS the
uniqueness guard — a second establish for the same nullifier fails
("already in use"). Same seed/layout as `establish_credit_root`.

accounts.name: authority
docs: The trusted operator. Must be `INTERIM_ROOT_AUTHORITY` — the same
authority trusted to post interim World ID roots. It pays rent and
attests the off-chain personhood verification.

instructions.name: establish_root
docs: Phase 2 — trustless World ID root writer. Permissionless: anyone with a
valid SP1-Helios proof may advance the cached root (no authority gate,
unlike `post_interim_root`).

accounts.name: root_cache
docs: The singleton World ID root cache — the SAME PDA `post_interim_root`
writes. `init_if_needed` so the FIRST trustless write can create it if no
interim root was ever posted; subsequent writes mutate in place.
`init_if_needed` re-init safety (mirrors set_standby_reserve /
post_interim_root): the PDA address is DETERMINISTIC (single fixed seed),
so an attacker cannot substitute a different account here, and the handler
writes every field unconditionally on each call — there is no partially
re-initialized state. The monotonic-head guard below is what prevents a
stale/replayed older root from overwriting a newer cached one.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays rent on first creation. Anyone may pay; posting is permissionless —
the SP1 proof, not the signer identity, authorizes the write.

accounts.name: swig
docs: Position 0 — REQUIRED at this index by Swig's ProgramExec authority
validator. When a Swig::SignV2 follows this instruction in the same
transaction, Swig's on-chain validator inspects accounts[0..1] of the
preceding instruction and rejects unless they're [swig, swig_wallet].
We additionally enforce `swig.key() == vault.swig_address` via the
`address` constraint so a caller cannot pass an arbitrary Swig account
in here — defense in depth: even if Swig's own validation changes in a
future program upgrade, this vault keeps its own invariant.
deserialize or dereference it.

accounts.name: swig_wallet_address
docs: Position 1 — required by Swig's ProgramExec validator (see `swig`).
The Swig wallet address is the PDA owning the SPL token ATA being
debited; it is derived under the Swig program at
`["swig-wallet-address", swig_pubkey]`.
We independently verify the canonical derivation via Anchor's `seeds`
+ `seeds::program` constraint. If a caller supplied a fake account, our
program rejects before any Swig CPI runs — we do not rely on Swig
catching it downstream.

accounts.name: vault_usdc_ata
docs: The swig wallet's USDC ATA — read live to enforce the reservation
invariant per V0.3 Decision 1: post-withdrawal balance must remain
at or above `vault.outstanding_locked_amount`. The token account's
`owner` field must match the swig_wallet_address PDA.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: buyer's passkey signature via the SIMD-0075 precompile sibling.

instructions.name: init_graph_config
docs: Task 1 — create the GraphConfig singleton (admin spine). Only
INTERIM_ROOT_AUTHORITY may create it; `init` so it exists exactly once.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Only the interim/admin root authority may create the singleton.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: The Dexter session authority to bind to this vault. Must sign init, so
a vault can only be created bound to an authority that consented. This
key may later mutate `pending_voucher_count` (settle_voucher /
force_release) — and only this key. It can never move funds.

accounts.name: vault_usdc_ata
docs: The swig wallet's USDC ATA — read to enforce the vault-balance
self-check per V0.3 Decision 1. The handler cross-checks that this
token account's `owner` field equals the canonical swig wallet PDA
derived below so a caller cannot pass an unrelated funded ATA.

accounts.name: session
docs: V6: the per-counterparty SessionAccount PDA whose meter graduates. The
session moved OUT of `vault.active_session` into this PDA at
`[SESSION_SEED, vault, allowed_counterparty]`. `mut` — the graduation
mutates `current_outstanding`, `crystallized_cumulative`, and
`last_locked_sequence` in place.

accounts.name: claim
docs: Claim PDA created in this instruction.

accounts.name: seller_holder
docs: Holder at creation — the seller who collected the voucher.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Fee gate (matches settle_tab_voucher's discipline). Recorded on the
vault at init; only this key authorizes the accumulator mutation.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays rent for the claim PDA. May be dexter_authority, may be a
separate funder. Not security-load-bearing.

accounts.name: vault
docs: vault. It CANNOT be loaded as `Account<'info, Vault>` (no version byte,
different field shapes), so it is validated entirely by hand in
`decode_legacy_vault`: exact length 151 or 183, Vault discriminator,
full frozen-layout cursor decode. Owner-gated to this program.

accounts.name: authority
docs: The gate signer. For a 183-byte vault: must equal the STORED
`dexter_authority` (which is preserved into V6). For a 151-byte vault
(no stored authority exists): must equal `LEGACY_MIGRATE_ADMIN`, and is
stamped as the new `dexter_authority`.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Funds the rent top-up for the grow (151/183 → 279 bytes).

accounts.name: vault
docs: V3 struct, so it CANNOT be loaded as `Account<'info, Vault>` (the
auto-deserialize would over-run the buffer on the `active_session`
field). We validate it manually in the handler: discriminator match,
version == 2, and `dexter_authority` ownership. We also require it be
owned by this program before touching its bytes.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Must equal the vault's recorded `dexter_authority`. Same authority that
gates `settle_voucher` / `rotate_dexter_authority`. Validated by hand
against the raw buffer (no `has_one`, since the vault is untyped here).

accounts.name: payer
docs: Funds the rent for the 16 extra bytes the realloc adds. A separate
signer so the authority key need not hold lamports.

accounts.name: vault
docs: (305 bytes on mainnet). We deliberately do NOT load it as
`Account<'info, Vault>`, because Anchor would auto-deserialize it against
the CURRENT (larger, V4) struct and over-run the still-V3-sized buffer. We
validate it by hand in the handler: discriminator match, version == 3, and
`dexter_authority` ownership. We also require it be owned by this program
before touching its bytes.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Must equal the vault's recorded `dexter_authority`. Same authority that
gates `settle_voucher` / `rotate_dexter_authority`. Validated by hand
against the decoded V3 struct (no `has_one`, since the vault is untyped
here).

accounts.name: payer
docs: Funds the rent for the 36 extra bytes the realloc adds. A separate signer
so the authority key need not hold lamports.

accounts.name: vault
docs: We deliberately do NOT load it as `Account<'info, Vault>`, because Anchor
would auto-deserialize it against the CURRENT (larger, V5) struct and
over-run the still-V4-sized buffer. We validate it by hand in the handler:
discriminator match, version == 4, and `dexter_authority` ownership. We
also require it be owned by this program before touching its bytes.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Must equal the vault's recorded `dexter_authority`. Same authority that
gates `settle_voucher` / `rotate_dexter_authority`. Validated by hand
against the decoded V4 struct (no `has_one`, since the vault is untyped
here).

accounts.name: payer
docs: Funds the rent for the extra bytes the realloc adds. A separate signer so
the authority key need not hold lamports.

accounts.name: vault
docs: deserialized as the current (V6) Vault. owner-gated to this program.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Authority gate — must equal the decoded vault.dexter_authority.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Receives the refunded rent (the Vault shrinks).

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Authority gate — must equal the decoded vault.dexter_authority.

accounts.name: session
docs: The new session PDA, created here to hold the carried live session.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Funds the session PDA rent; receives the vault's freed (shrink) rent.

instructions.name: open_standby
docs: Task 5 — `open_standby` → GROW an edge: (re)set a child node's `RateCap`
under its parent. The parent's controller signs (GROW consent); the new
cap must fit under the parent's (JIT — no reservation total).

accounts.name: node
docs: The CHILD node whose `RateCap` is being (re)set. Must already be parented
to `parent_node` (enforced in the handler via `node.parent`).

accounts.name: parent_node
docs: The granting PARENT node. Read for its cap (the upper bound) and its
controller (the GROW signer).

accounts.name: parent_controller
docs: The parent's controller — GROW consent. Address-constrained to
`parent_node.controller`, so an attacker cannot substitute an arbitrary
signer. Requiring it to SIGN binds this re-cap to the parent's authority.

accounts.name: graph_config
docs: The graph admin spine — read for the global pause flag. Re-grant is an
exposure-(re)setting verb → gated by the pause.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Read-only: this instruction proves passkey control and mutates NOTHING.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: passkey signature via the SIMD-0075 precompile sibling instruction.

accounts.name: credit_root
docs: The human's permanent credit identity. `mut` because `event_count` is the
monotonic seq we both seed the event with and then increment. The seed
uses the stored nullifier (canonical bump), so this is the canonical PDA.

accounts.name: vault
docs: The per-agent vault this event concerns. Read-only here. It must be the
canonical vault for `derive_identity_claim(credit_root.nullifier, agent_id)`
— the handler enforces that weld below.

accounts.name: credit_event
docs: The new credit event, keyed to the HUMAN (nullifier) at the current seq.
`init` (NOT init_if_needed): each seq is written exactly once; a replay at
the same seq would hit "already in use". The seed binds it to the
nullifier + the current `event_count`.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays rent for the new CreditEvent.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Receives the new `active_session`. Mutated, no signer required: the
passkey signature embedded in the args (verified via the SIMD-0075
precompile sibling) is what authorizes the mutation.

accounts.name: vault_usdc_ata
docs: The swig wallet's USDC ATA — read live to enforce the overcommit
invariant per V0.3 Decision 1: the new session's max_amount plus
the existing outstanding_locked_amount must fit within actual USDC
balance. When present, the ATA's `owner` field is verified against the
canonical swig wallet PDA.
OPTIONAL: a $0 credit-only vault never receives a deposit, so its USDC
ATA is never created (an ATA is born on first incoming transfer). Such a
vault is backed entirely by its standby credit line, not its own USDC.
When the account is absent (None — the program-ID sentinel), own-USDC is
treated as 0 and backing is the available standby credit alone. Omitting
it can only ever UNDERSTATE backing (own-USDC defaults to 0), so it is
safe: a self-funded vault that needs its balance counted MUST pass the
ATA, and omission only ever yields a smaller permitted session.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: instruction in the transaction MUST be a secp256r1_verify call whose
signed message is `authenticatorData || sha256(clientDataJSON)` and
whose `clientDataJSON.challenge` decodes to sha256(registration_message).

accounts.name: session
docs: The per-counterparty session PDA. init_if_needed: created on the first
register to this counterparty, OVERWRITTEN (replace-in-place) on a
subsequent register. The seed binds it to (vault, allowed_counterparty),
so it cannot be redirected. Re-init safety (SOL-010): the handler fully
overwrites every passkey-endorsed scope field (Task 5).

accounts.name: payer
docs: Funds the session PDA rent on first creation.

accounts.name: swig
docs: Position 0 — required at this index by Swig's ProgramExec validator.
This is the USER's swig, address-constrained to `vault.swig_address`
(the OPPOSITE of draw_credit's financier swig). The following SignV2
spends the USER's swig_wallet ATA to repay the financier.

accounts.name: swig_wallet_address
docs: Position 1 — required by Swig's ProgramExec validator. Derives from the
USER's swig, so the following SignV2 spends the USER's swig_wallet ATA.

accounts.name: vault
docs: The USER's vault. Bound to the drawing node via `vault.node`; carries the
swig binding (`vault.swig_address`) above. Read-only — credit state now
lives on the PrincipalNode graph, not the Vault.

accounts.name: drawing_node
docs: The PrincipalNode whose `borrowed` is paid down. Must equal `vault.node`.
The leaf of the authenticated ancestor chain the repay decrements. Mutated.

accounts.name: graph_config
docs: Graph admin spine — read for the runtime `max_depth_override` only. The
PAUSE flag does NOT gate repay (de-risking verbs stay live even paused).

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: The orchestration authority driving the paydown. Matches draw_credit's
fee gate exactly.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: instruction is the swig::SignV2 that introspects this one.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Mutated to decrement `live_session_count`. No signer required; the passkey
signature in the args is the authorization.

accounts.name: session
docs: The session PDA being revoked. mut (we clear it in place). bump from stored.
The seed binds it to (vault, allowed_counterparty), so it cannot be
redirected to a foreign counterparty's session.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: instruction in the transaction MUST be a secp256r1_verify call.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Must equal the vault's CURRENT `dexter_authority`. Only the current
authority can hand off to a new one — so the session-master key can be
rotated without bricking existing vaults.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: CURRENT passkey signature via the SIMD-0075 precompile sibling.

instructions.name: seize_ancestor
docs: Task 8 — RUNG 3 `seize_ancestor` → the LIABILITY CASCADE. After a partial
self-seize leaves a `shortfall` on the defaulted leaf, the financier
seizes an ANCESTOR's pre-authorized collateral (its own swig) to cover a
slice of that shortfall, decrementing `subtree_draw` up the authenticated
chain. Model A (full liability, no per-edge cap); the total is structurally
bounded by the root ceiling.

accounts.name: ancestor_swig
docs: Position 0 — the ANCESTOR's swig (required at this index by swig's
ProgramExec validator). The cascade money comes from HERE. Mirrors
draw_credit's `financier_swig`; the following SignV2 spends THIS swig's
wallet ATA → financier.

accounts.name: ancestor_swig_wallet_address
docs: Position 1 — the ancestor swig's swig_wallet_address PDA. The following
SignV2 spends THIS ATA's owner.

accounts.name: ancestor_node
docs: The ANCESTOR PrincipalNode whose pre-authorized collateral covers a slice
of the defaulter's shortfall. READ-ONLY (not `mut`): its `subtree_draw` is
decremented by the authenticated Decrement traverse below (it is one hop of
the chain in `remaining_accounts`), so making it a `mut` named account too
would clobber the traverse's write at instruction exit. Used here only to
(a) identify which ancestor pays and (b) prove genuine-ancestry — its key
MUST appear in the authenticated chain.

accounts.name: defaulted_node
docs: The DEFAULTED leaf carrying the uncovered `shortfall`. Mutated: `shortfall`
(and `borrowed` / `subtree_draw`) fall by `args.amount`; the deadline
clears when the shortfall reaches 0 (fully covered).

accounts.name: graph_config
docs: Graph admin spine — read for the runtime `max_depth_override` only. The
PAUSE flag does NOT gate the cascade (a de-risking verb stays live even
paused; the Decrement traverse also passes through a frozen subtree).

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: The orchestration authority driving the cascade. There is no Vault here to
bind it to (the defaulter is a PrincipalNode, not a Vault); the real
consent is the deadline + the ANCESTOR swig's cascade marker + the
authenticated ancestry. Signer-only (mirrors the financier in draw_credit,
which is likewise not passkey-verified in v1).

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: instruction is the swig::SignV2 that spends the ancestor swig's ATA.

accounts.name: swig
docs: Position 0 — required at this index by Swig's ProgramExec validator.
This is the USER's swig, address-constrained to `vault.swig_address`
(same as repay_credit; the OPPOSITE of draw_credit's financier swig).
The following SignV2 spends the USER's swig_wallet ATA to pay the
financier the seized slice.

accounts.name: swig_wallet_address
docs: Position 1 — required by Swig's ProgramExec validator. Derives from the
USER's swig, so the following SignV2 spends the USER's swig_wallet ATA.

accounts.name: vault
docs: The USER's vault. Bound to the drawing node via `vault.node`; carries the
swig binding (`vault.swig_address`) above. Read-only — credit state lives
on the PrincipalNode graph, not the Vault.

accounts.name: drawing_node
docs: The PrincipalNode being liquidated. Must equal `vault.node`. `borrowed`
becomes the uncovered `shortfall` here; `subtree_draw` falls by the seized
slice; the deadline clears iff fully self-covered. Mutated.

accounts.name: collateral_ata
docs: The drawing node's swig_wallet USDC ATA — read live so the partial seize
computes `seized_from_self = min(borrowed, available_collateral)` against
the REAL on-chain balance. The `owner` is cross-checked against the
canonical swig wallet PDA (lifted from register_session_key) so a caller
cannot smuggle a foreign funded ATA to over/under-state the partial.

accounts.name: graph_config
docs: Graph admin spine — read for the runtime `max_depth_override` only. The
PAUSE flag does NOT gate seize (de-risking verbs stay live even paused).

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: The orchestration authority driving the liquidation. Matches
repay_credit / draw_credit's gate exactly. The financier is NOT
passkey-verified in v1 — the deadline + dexter_authority are the gate.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: instruction is the swig::SignV2 that introspects this one.

instructions.name: set_freeze
docs: Task 12 — `set_freeze`: set/clear a node's `frozen` flag. A proven STRICT
ancestor (the rooted root of the target's chain, established via the
read-only authenticated traverse over `remaining_accounts`) authorizes it;
a node may not freeze/unfreeze itself. Freeze halts exposure-increasing
verbs beneath the node; de-risking stays live through a frozen subtree.

accounts.name: target_node
docs: The node whose `frozen` flag is being set. Mutated.

accounts.name: ancestor_node
docs: The proven STRICT ancestor authorizing the freeze — must be the rooted
ROOT of `target_node`'s chain (the handler requires the traverse's root ==
this account). Read-only; the traverse persists its (unchanged) state via
the matching `remaining_accounts` entry.

accounts.name: ancestor_controller
docs: The ancestor's controller — bound to `ancestor_node.controller` and
required to SIGN (freeze is an authority the ancestor's controller wields).

accounts.name: graph_config
docs: The graph admin spine — read for the runtime `max_depth_override` (the
traverse's depth ceiling). Freeze is NOT pause-gated (an operator must be
able to freeze a subtree even on a paused graph).

instructions.name: set_pause
docs: Task 12 — `set_pause`: toggle the global `paused` flag (two-key
asymmetry). PAUSE accepts the hot pause_authority OR the cold admin;
UNPAUSE accepts the cold admin ONLY (`OnlyAdminMayUnpause`). Pause gates
only exposure-increasing verbs; de-risking stays live.

accounts.name: graph_config
docs: The graph admin spine — mutated (`paused` / `paused_at` / `pause_reason`).

accounts.name: authority
docs: The caller. PAUSE accepts the hot pause_authority OR the cold admin;
UNPAUSE accepts the cold admin ONLY (enforced in the handler).

instructions.name: set_standby_reserve
docs: Task 5 — `set_standby_reserve` → SET-ROOT-CEILING: set a ROOT node's
`RateCap.ceiling` (the per-person aggregate cap). The root controller
signs; the new ceiling may not drop below `subtree_draw`.

accounts.name: node
docs: The ROOT node whose aggregate ceiling is being set. Mutated:
`cap.ceiling`. Must be a root (`parent == None`), enforced in-handler.

accounts.name: controller
docs: The node's controller — consent. Address-constrained to `node.controller`
and required to SIGN: only the root's controller may set its ceiling.

accounts.name: vault
docs: The dexter-vault PDA — initialized by initialize_vault, mutated here
(we set swig_address).

accounts.name: fee_payer
docs: The fee payer + role-0 bootstrap authority. MUST be a tx-level signer.

accounts.name: swig_account
docs: The Swig state account, derived as `findProgramAddress([swig_id], swig_program)`.
Swig will initialize it during the CreateV1 CPI.

accounts.name: swig_wallet_address
docs: The Swig wallet PDA (different from swig_account — it's the spending
authority address). CPI'd into by CreateV1.

accounts.name: swig_program
docs: The Swig program itself.

accounts.name: system_program
docs: System program. Declared readonly at the outer-ix level — Solana's
runtime demotes the System Program to readonly anyway (program-id
accounts cannot be writable in the outer transaction). All inner CPIs
(both CreateV1 and AddAuthority) therefore pass System Program as
`AccountMeta::new_readonly(...)` to avoid privilege escalation.
`system_program::CreateAccount` itself doesn't require the System
Program account to be writable — it operates on `from`/`to`.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: Instructions sysvar — read by verify_passkey_signed.

accounts.name: swig
docs: Position 0 — required at this index by Swig's ProgramExec validator.

accounts.name: swig_wallet_address
docs: Position 1 — required by Swig's ProgramExec validator.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Accumulator vault. Mutated to decrement outstanding_locked_amount
and advance total_settled_amount. Per V0.3 Decision 7, the
`active_session` field is NOT read here.

accounts.name: holder
docs: Must equal claim.current_holder. This signature is the sole
authority for the settlement.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Fee gate (matches settle_tab_voucher's discipline). MUST be `mut`:
`close = dexter_authority` on the claim credits the reclaimed rent to
this account, mutating its lamports — without `mut` the runtime rejects
settle with "instruction changed the balance of a read-only account"
(matches close_session, whose dexter_authority is also `mut`).

accounts.name: swig
docs: Position 0 — REQUIRED at this index by Swig's ProgramExec authority
validator. When a Swig::SignV2 follows this instruction in the same
transaction, Swig's on-chain validator inspects accounts[0..1] of
the preceding instruction and rejects unless they're
[swig, swig_wallet], AND that the preceding instruction's data
starts with a registered marker discriminator.
Also bound to `vault.swig_address` via the Anchor `address`
constraint, so a caller cannot pass an arbitrary Swig account here.
deserialize or dereference it.

accounts.name: swig_wallet_address
docs: Position 1 — required by Swig's ProgramExec validator (see `swig`).
The Swig wallet address is the PDA owning the SPL token ATA being
debited; derived under the Swig program at
`["swig-wallet-address", swig_pubkey]`.

accounts.name: session
docs: V6: the per-counterparty SessionAccount PDA this voucher settles
against. The session moved OUT of `vault.active_session` into this
PDA at `[SESSION_SEED, vault, allowed_counterparty]`. The seed binds
it to (vault, the seller named in the voucher), so a caller cannot
settle against a foreign counterparty's session. `mut` — we advance
the `spent` odometer and release `current_outstanding` in place.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Must equal `vault.dexter_authority` — only the recorded authority
can drive the gate-counter decrement. The buyer's session-key
signature is what authorizes the SPEND amount; this signer is what
authorizes the counter mutation. Same model as the existing
`settle_voucher`. NOTE: this signer does NOT sign the Swig transfer
in [N+1] — that's signed by the swig wallet PDA via Swig's
ProgramExec authority, gated by the vault program being the
ProgramExec authority on the Swig.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Must equal the `dexter_authority` recorded on the vault at init.
`has_one` enforces this — closing Finding B (previously any signer
could mutate the counter).

accounts.name: session
docs: V6: the per-counterparty SessionAccount PDA whose revolving meter rises
at tab-open. OPTIONAL because only the `increment == true` (tab-open)
path touches a session — the `increment == false` (close) path is a
bare `pending_voucher_count` decrement that never reads a session, so a
caller closing a counter need not pass one. When present, the seed binds
it to (vault, allowed_counterparty); `mut` because the RISE seam writes
`current_outstanding`. The handler REQUIRES `Some(live)` on the
increment path (you cannot raise a meter that isn't there).

accounts.name: current_holder
docs: Must equal the claim's current_holder. This is the sole authority
for transferring the claim per the reservation semantics doc
(Reservation 2).

errors.name: CoolingOffNotElapsed
errors.msg: Cooling-off period has not elapsed

errors.name: PendingVouchersExist
errors.msg: Pending vouchers must settle before withdrawal can finalize

errors.name: NoPendingWithdrawal
errors.msg: No pending withdrawal request

errors.name: PasskeyVerificationFailed
errors.msg: Passkey signature verification failed

errors.name: InvalidVoucherSignature
errors.msg: Voucher signature does not match Dexter session key

errors.name: ForceReleaseTooEarly
errors.msg: force_release grace period has not elapsed

errors.name: NothingToRelease
errors.msg: No stuck voucher to force-release

errors.name: UnsupportedVaultVersion
errors.msg: Vault account version is not supported by this program

errors.name: SessionAlreadyActive
errors.msg: A session is already active on this vault and has not expired

errors.name: SessionExpiryInPast
errors.msg: Session expiry must be in the future

errors.name: SessionCapZero
errors.msg: Session max_amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: NoActiveSession
errors.msg: No active session to revoke

errors.name: SessionPubkeyMismatch
errors.msg: Revocation message session pubkey does not match the active session

errors.name: RevolvingCapacityExceeded
errors.msg: Opening this tab would exceed the session's revolving capacity

errors.name: RevolvingCapacityZero
errors.msg: max_revolving_capacity must be greater than zero

errors.name: LockRangeAlreadyClaimed
errors.msg: Voucher's cumulative_amount does not advance the XOR frontier (already covered by spent or crystallized_cumulative)

errors.name: LockWouldOvercommitVault
errors.msg: Locking this voucher would push outstanding_locked_amount above vault USDC balance

errors.name: WithdrawalWouldViolateReservation
errors.msg: Finalizing this withdrawal would bring vault balance below outstanding_locked_amount

errors.name: SessionWouldOvercommitVault
errors.msg: Registering this session would push max_amount + outstanding_locked_amount above vault USDC balance + available standby credit

errors.name: SessionAccountsNotSorted
errors.msg: remaining_accounts not in strict ascending pubkey order (dedup/order)

errors.name: SessionAccountForeign
errors.msg: a passed session account belongs to a different vault

errors.name: SessionAccountMisderived
errors.msg: a passed account is not the canonical session PDA for its counterparty

errors.name: IncompleteSessionSet
errors.msg: live sibling sessions passed != live_session_count (incomplete set)

errors.name: SessionCountAtMax
errors.msg: vault already has 255 live sessions; revoke one before registering

errors.name: RecoveryBeforeMaturity
errors.msg: holder_recovery_at must be strictly greater than maturity_at when both are set

errors.name: WithdrawalWouldViolatePin
errors.msg: Withdrawal would violate the credit pin (borrowed amount is reserved).

errors.name: BorrowRecoveryTooEarly
errors.msg: Borrow recovery deadline has not passed yet.

errors.name: NothingBorrowed
errors.msg: Nothing is borrowed on this vault.

errors.name: SessionAccountNotWritable
errors.msg: an expired sibling session must be passed WRITABLE so the register-time sweep can clear it

errors.name: SessionStillLive
errors.msg: session is still live (version != 0); revoke_session_key it before close_session can reclaim its rent

errors.name: NotALegacyVault
errors.msg: account is not a recognized pre-version-byte legacy vault (expected exactly 151-byte V1 or 183-byte V1.5 with the Vault discriminator)

errors.name: ClaimNotTerminal
errors.msg: claim is still Pending (a live obligation); only Settled or Abandoned claims can be closed for rent reclaim

errors.name: WorldIdVerificationFailed
errors.msg: World ID v4 pi2 (OPRFNullifier) groth16 proof failed to verify against the production verifying key

errors.name: WorldIdRootMismatch
errors.msg: Proof merkle_root does not match the cached World ID root

errors.name: WorldIdContextMismatch
errors.msg: Proof rp_id/action does not match the Dexter context

errors.name: WorldIdCredentialMismatch
errors.msg: Proof credential/network signals (issuer_schema_id/cred_pk/depth/oprf_pk) do not match the pinned World ID values

errors.name: NotInterimRootAuthority
errors.msg: Caller is not the interim-root authority

errors.name: VaultNotRootedToCredit
errors.msg: Vault identity_claim is not derived from this CreditRoot's nullifier + agent_id

errors.name: Sp1ProofTooShort
errors.msg: SP1 proof bytes are too short to contain the 4-byte vk-hash prefix

errors.name: Sp1VerifyFailed
errors.msg: SP1-Helios Groth16 proof failed to verify against the pinned verifying key

errors.name: Sp1PublicValuesMalformed
errors.msg: SP1-Helios committed public values are malformed (ABI decode out of bounds)

errors.name: WorldIdSlotNotFound
errors.msg: No proven storage slot matched the pinned World ID registry address + latest-root slot key

errors.name: BeaconHeadNotMonotonic
errors.msg: New beacon head is older than the cached root's block_number (stale/replayed root)

errors.name: BeaconHeadOverflow
errors.msg: Proven beacon head (newHead) does not fit in a u64

errors.name: GraphPaused
errors.msg: The graph is paused; exposure-increasing verbs are disabled

errors.name: NotAdminAuthority
errors.msg: Caller is not the GraphConfig admin authority

errors.name: NotPauseAuthority
errors.msg: Caller is not the GraphConfig pause authority

errors.name: OnlyAdminMayUnpause
errors.msg: Only the admin authority may unpause

errors.name: DelegationDepthExceeded
errors.msg: Delegation depth would exceed the configured maximum

errors.name: UnauthenticatedAncestorChain
errors.msg: An ancestor in the chain is not authenticated

errors.name: AncestorChainNotRooted
errors.msg: The ancestor chain is not rooted

errors.name: DrawWouldExceedCeiling
errors.msg: Draw would exceed the node's ceiling

errors.name: DrawWouldExceedBucket
errors.msg: Draw would exceed the node's rate-limit bucket

errors.name: ChildCapExceedsParent
errors.msg: A child cap may not exceed its parent's cap

errors.name: NotNodeController
errors.msg: Caller is not the controller of this node

errors.name: ReleaseExceedsSubtreeDraw
errors.msg: Release would exceed the subtree's outstanding draw

errors.name: NodeFrozenErr
errors.msg: This node is frozen

errors.name: NodeHasOutstandingDraw
errors.msg: Node has outstanding draw and cannot perform this operation

errors.name: RootAttestationMissing
errors.msg: Root attestation is missing

errors.name: NodeAlreadyRooted
errors.msg: This node is already rooted

errors.name: ChildCountAtMax
errors.msg: This node already has the maximum number of children

errors.name: RateCapZero
errors.msg: Rate cap must be greater than zero

errors.name: WalletNodeMismatch
errors.msg: Wallet does not match the node

errors.name: DebtTransferMismatch
errors.msg: Debt-transfer amounts do not reconcile

errors.name: ShortfallAlreadyCovered
errors.msg: This shortfall has already been covered

errors.name: NotAnAncestorOfDefaulter
errors.msg: Caller is not an ancestor of the defaulting node

errors.name: NoUncoveredShortfall
errors.msg: There is no uncovered shortfall to cascade

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; challenge must be sha256(op_message).

fields.name: authenticator_data
docs: WebAuthn authenticatorData (37+ bytes).

fields.name: nullifier
docs: The human nullifier — the `CreditRoot` PDA seed component. The supplied
`credit_root` account is bound to `[CREDIT_ROOT_SEED, nullifier]`, so an
attacker cannot substitute an arbitrary account.

types.name: CapChanged
docs: `open_standby` (grow), `close_standby` (cut-edge) and `set_standby_reserve`
(set-root-ceiling) — a node's `RateCap`/edge changed. Carries only the
affected node; consumers re-fetch the node for the new cap.

fields.name: allowed_counterparty
docs: The counterparty whose CLEARED session PDA is being closed. Goes in the
args so the accounts struct can re-derive the PDA seed — the account
body's own copy of this field is zeroed on a cleared session, so the
seed derivation is the only binding.

types.name: CollateralSeized
docs: `seize_collateral` (self-seize) and `seize_ancestor` (the RUNG-3 cascade) —
`amount` of collateral was seized against `node`'s debt, rolling down the
authenticated chain to `root`.

fields.name: node_id
docs: Stable identity + PDA seed component for the new node.

fields.name: cap
docs: The new node's draw authority (velocity bucket + optional hard ceiling).

fields.name: parent
docs: Some ⇒ this is a delegate; the parent's controller must sign and the
child cap must fit under the parent's. None ⇒ an anonymous root-less node.

types.name: CreditDrawn
docs: `draw_credit` — a leaf booked new debt and the authenticated chain rolled it
up to the ceiling-root. `depth` = ancestor hops above the leaf (0 ⇒ a depth-1
leaf-root drawing on itself).

types.name: CreditEvent
docs: A single credit event keyed to the HUMAN (nullifier), not a vault.
PDA: seeds = [CREDIT_EVENT_SEED, nullifier, &seq.to_le_bytes()].

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version. First field per house style.

fields.name: bump
docs: Canonical bump for this PDA.

fields.name: nullifier
docs: The human nullifier this event is keyed to (mirrors the CreditRoot seed).

fields.name: seq
docs: Monotonic sequence number within the human's credit history.

fields.name: vault
docs: The per-agent vault this event concerns (advisory; credit follows N).

fields.name: recorded_at
docs: Unix time this event was recorded on-chain.

fields.name: kind
docs: Opaque event payload kind (extended by the credit product).

fields.name: amount
docs: USDC amount (or product-defined unit) associated with this event.

types.name: CreditRepaid
docs: `repay_credit` — outstanding draw was paid down (leaf + every ancestor
decremented in lockstep). `new_borrowed` is the leaf's remaining debt.

types.name: CreditRoot
docs: One-human-one-root. PDA: seeds = [CREDIT_ROOT_SEED, nullifier]. `init`
(NOT init_if_needed) → existence IS the uniqueness guard. PERMANENT: there
is no close instruction; this is the human's durable credit identity.

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version. First field per house style.

fields.name: bump
docs: Canonical bump for this PDA.

fields.name: nullifier
docs: The verified World ID nullifier N (the seed). Deterministic per
(human, DEXTER_RP_ID, DEXTER_ACTION).

fields.name: established_at
docs: Unix time this CreditRoot was established on-chain.

fields.name: event_count
docs: Running count of credit events recorded against this human (event seq).

fields.name: amount
docs: USDC drawn from the financier's swig to the seller. The following SignV2
must transfer EXACTLY this amount, or the draw is rejected.

fields.name: recovery_window_seconds
docs: Seconds from now until the financier may `seize_collateral`. Sets
`borrow_recovery_at` on the FIRST draw only.

fields.name: new_nullifier
docs: `Some(N)` ⇒ acquire the human `CreditRoot` at `[CREDIT_ROOT_SEED, N]` while
cutting the parent edge. `None` ⇒ cut the edge and keep any existing
attestation (the node may stand root-less or retain a prior root).

types.name: EstablishCreditRootArgs
docs: The invention: a World ID v4 π₂ (OPRFNullifier) proof plus its 15 public
inputs. The verifier folds the proof against the embedded VK; the handler
then binds the public inputs to the cached root + Dexter context and `init`s
the per-human `CreditRoot` keyed by the verified nullifier.

fields.name: proof_a
docs: G1, negated + big-endian (groth16-solana input convention).

fields.name: proof_b
docs: G2, `x_c1 || x_c0 || y_c1 || y_c0`, each big-endian.

fields.name: proof_c
docs: G1, big-endian, NOT negated.

fields.name: public_inputs
docs: The 15 π₂ public inputs, each a 32-byte big-endian bn254 field element.
`public_inputs[0]` is the nullifier (the CreditRoot seed).

types.name: EstablishCreditRootTrustedArgs
docs: Trusted (off-chain-verified) personhood → CreditRoot establishment.
Sibling of `establish_credit_root`. That instruction verifies a World ID v4
π₂ (OPRFNullifier) groth16 proof on-chain (trustless). THIS instruction
writes the same `CreditRoot` PDA from an authority-attested nullifier whose
personhood was verified OFF-CHAIN (e.g. a v3 Orb proof checked against World
ID's own verifier), with NO on-chain groth16 step.
It is the deliberate "trusted-today → trustless-later" bridge: a CreditRoot
established here is byte-identical in layout to a groth16-established one
(same PDA seed = the nullifier, same fields), so every downstream reader
(`readCreditRoot`, `record_credit_event`) treats it identically — the ONLY
difference is `version = 2` (trusted) vs `1` (trustless groth16), which
records HOW personhood was attested without changing the credit machinery.
Gated to `INTERIM_ROOT_AUTHORITY` (the program's operator/upgrade authority)
— the on-chain embodiment of "the facilitator asserts this proof checked
out". Like `post_interim_root`, this is a Phase-1 trusted leg; the trustless
`establish_credit_root` remains the canonical path.

fields.name: nullifier
docs: The off-chain-verified World ID nullifier. Becomes the CreditRoot PDA
seed AND the stored `nullifier` — the durable one-human-one-root key.

types.name: EstablishRootArgs
docs: Phase 2 — the TRUSTLESS World ID root writer. An SP1-Helios Groth16 proof
attests an Ethereum light-client state transition; we verify it and read the
World ID registry's `_latestRoot` out of the proven storage slots, then cache
it as a `RootSource::Sp1Helios` root.
Trust-model difference vs `post_interim_root`: this instruction is
PERMISSIONLESS. There is no authority gate — anyone holding a valid
SP1-Helios proof may advance the cache. The proof IS the authorization.

fields.name: proof
docs: SP1 proof bytes (`SP1ProofWithPublicValues::bytes()`): a 4-byte groth16
vk-hash prefix followed by the 256-byte Groth16 proof.

fields.name: public_values
docs: Raw ABI-encoded `ProofOutputs` (the SP1-Helios committed public values).

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; challenge must be sha256(operation_message).

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; challenge must be sha256(operation_message).

types.name: GraphConfig
docs: Admin spine for the recourse-rooted delegation graph. Singleton PDA:
seeds = [GRAPH_CONFIG_SEED]. `init` (NOT init_if_needed) → existence IS the
uniqueness guard; only INTERIM_ROOT_AUTHORITY may create it. Holds the
runtime tunables read by later tasks (pause flags, max-depth override).

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version. First field per house style.

fields.name: bump
docs: Canonical bump for this singleton PDA.

fields.name: admin_authority
docs: Cold key: may pause, unpause, and change tunables.

fields.name: pause_authority
docs: Hot key: may pause ONLY (never unpause, never tune).

fields.name: paused
docs: Global pause flag. Gates exposure-increasing verbs only (enforced later).

fields.name: paused_at
docs: Unix time of the last pause (0 if never paused).

fields.name: pause_reason
docs: Opaque reason code for the last pause.

fields.name: max_depth_override
docs: Runtime max-depth override. 0 ⇒ use the MAX_DEPTH const.

fields.name: admin_authority
docs: Cold key recorded as `admin_authority` (pause + unpause + tunables).

fields.name: pause_authority
docs: Hot key recorded as `pause_authority` (pause ONLY).

fields.name: max_depth_override
docs: Runtime max-depth override. 0 ⇒ use the MAX_DEPTH const.

fields.name: cooling_off_seconds
docs: Withdrawal cooling-off in seconds. Zero = instant. See §7.1 of the v2
design doc for why this tightened from i64 to u32.

fields.name: identity_claim
docs: Operator-defined opaque identity bytes. The protocol doesn't interpret
these. Dexter writes a Supabase UUID into the first 16 bytes and zeros
the rest; future operators may use whichever scheme they want.

fields.name: channel_id
docs: Channel id from the voucher payload (first 32 bytes of the 44-byte
signed message).

fields.name: cumulative_amount
docs: Voucher's cumulative amount. Must strictly exceed the XOR frontier
`max(session.spent, session.crystallized_cumulative)`.

fields.name: sequence_number
docs: Sequence number from the voucher payload. Updates
`session.last_locked_sequence` but is NOT load-bearing for replay
defense (the frontier guard is).

fields.name: voucher_hash
docs: sha256(voucher_message) — used as the third PDA seed for the claim.
The handler verifies this matches the actually-signed message before
trusting it as a seed.

fields.name: maturity_at
docs: Optional settlement maturity. If set, settle_locked_voucher rejects
before this time.

fields.name: holder_recovery_at
docs: Optional buyer-recovery deadline. If set with `maturity_at`, the
handler enforces `holder_recovery_at > maturity_at` per V0.3
Decision 4.

fields.name: allowed_counterparty
docs: V6: the seller this voucher's session is bound to — equals the session
PDA's `allowed_counterparty`. Carried in the args so the accounts
struct can re-derive the session PDA seed. Not part of the signed
voucher message (layout unchanged); the seed binding ties the lock to
the correct session.

types.name: LockedClaim
docs: Crystallized claim against vault USDC. Created by `lock_voucher`,
transferable via `transfer_lock_ownership`, settled by
`settle_locked_voucher`, reclaimed by `recover_abandoned_lock`. Independent
of any `SessionAccount` after creation — see V0.3 Decision 7. State machine
per V0.3 Decision 6: pending → {settled, abandoned}, both terminal one-way.

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version. v0.3 is the first version.

fields.name: vault
docs: Vault PDA this claim is reserved against. All reservation invariants
gate on this vault's `outstanding_locked_amount`.

fields.name: session_pubkey_at_lock
docs: Session pubkey that signed the voucher being crystallized. Snapshot at
lock time per V0.3 Decision 7; never re-read at settlement.

fields.name: voucher_hash
docs: Voucher payload hash — sha256 of the 44-byte canonical voucher message.
For audit / indexer correlation; not re-verified at settle.

fields.name: amount
docs: USDC amount this claim reserves against the vault. `delta` from
`voucher.cumulative_amount - session.crystallized_cumulative` at lock.

fields.name: created_at
docs: Wall-clock time the lock instruction landed.

fields.name: maturity_at
docs: Earliest time settlement may run. If None, claim is instantly
settleable.

fields.name: holder_recovery_at
docs: Earliest time the buyer's passkey may reclaim. If None, claim is
indefinitely buyer-irrevocable. Invariant: when both set, MUST satisfy
`holder_recovery_at > maturity_at` per V0.3 Decision 4.

fields.name: current_holder
docs: Current owner. Set at creation to the seller; mutated by
`transfer_lock_ownership` signed by the previous holder.

fields.name: status
docs: State machine status per V0.3 Decision 6.

fields.name: settled_at
docs: Set when status == Settled. None otherwise.

fields.name: recovered_at
docs: Set when status == Abandoned. None otherwise.

types.name: LockedClaimStatus
docs: Claim state per V0.3 Decision 6 state machine.

fields.name: live_counterparty
docs: The allowed_counterparty of the live active_session being carried. The
handler asserts this equals the decoded active_session.allowed_counterparty,
so the caller cannot redirect the created session PDA to another counterparty.

types.name: NodeAttached
docs: `attach_node` — the wallet↔node POSSESSION link (`Vault.node`) was set
(passkey-gated, link-once).

types.name: NodeDelegated
docs: `create_node` — a node was created. `parent == None` for a root-less node, or
`Some(parent)` for a delegate (GROW consent). The cap is the granted velocity
(`cap_rate` per `cap_period` secs) plus any aggregate `ceiling`.

types.name: NodeFrozen
docs: A node's `frozen` flag was toggled `by` an authority. (Struct defined per the
Task-11 spec; the freeze verb itself lands in a later task — no emitter yet.)

fields.name: cap
docs: The new `RateCap` to set on the child. Must fit under the parent's cap.

types.name: PrincipalNode
docs: One identity in the recourse-rooted delegation graph. Authority flows DOWN
(a controller grants children a `RateCap`); recourse flows UP (a defaulting
leaf's `shortfall` cascades to ancestors). PDA: seeds =
`[PRINCIPAL_NODE_SEED, node_id]` — the `node_id` is the stable identity, so a
node's address survives re-rooting (emancipation). `init` (NOT
init_if_needed): existence is the uniqueness guard per node_id.

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version. First field per house style.

fields.name: bump
docs: Canonical bump for this PDA.

fields.name: node_id
docs: Stable identity + PDA seed component. Survives re-rooting.

fields.name: controller
docs: The key that grants downward (creates children, sets their caps) and may
freeze this subtree.

fields.name: parent
docs: Parent node PDA. None ⇒ this is a root candidate (no upward recourse).

fields.name: root_attestation
docs: The attestation that roots this node (a `CreditRoot` PDA today; a future
attestation type later). None ⇒ not yet rooted.

fields.name: cap
docs: This node's draw authority — velocity bucket + optional hard ceiling.

fields.name: borrowed
docs: This node's OWN outstanding draw (the leaf accumulator). Rises on draw,
falls on repay/seize.

fields.name: subtree_draw
docs: Rolled-up subtree draw. Authoritative only at a ceiling-root; advisory
elsewhere. Maintained by the draw/repay traversal (Tasks 6/7).

fields.name: borrow_recovery_at
docs: Seize deadline armed on first draw, cleared when `borrowed` returns to 0.
None ⇒ nothing borrowed / no deadline armed.

fields.name: shortfall
docs: RUNG-3: uncovered remainder after a partial self-seize, awaiting the
ancestor cascade. 0 when no default is in progress. Lives on the DEFAULTED
leaf node; the cascade (Task 8) reads + reduces it.

fields.name: frozen
docs: Frozen subtree flag — set by the controller (or an ancestor) to halt
exposure-increasing verbs at/under this node.

fields.name: child_count
docs: Number of child nodes whose `parent` points here.

fields.name: accrued_fee
docs: Accrued fee accumulator. Accrual fields are present now; the accrual logic
lands later (rate_bps starts at 0 = no accrual).

fields.name: rate_bps
docs: Fee rate in basis points. 0 ⇒ no accrual (the layout-task default).

fields.name: last_accrual
docs: Unix time of the last fee accrual tick.

fields.name: challenge
docs: The 32-byte challenge to prove control over (e.g. a SIWX login nonce /
digest). The passkey must have signed `"siwx_login" || challenge`.

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; its `challenge` field must base64url-decode to
`sha256("siwx_login" || challenge)`.

types.name: RateCap
docs: A token-bucket rate limiter PLUS an optional hard ceiling for a node's draw
authority. `bucket` refills `rate_amount` every `period_secs`, capped at
`rate_amount * burst_multiple`; `last_refill` anchors the refill clock.
`ceiling` (when Some) is the absolute outstanding-draw cap independent of the
velocity bucket. Logic lands in later tasks (Task 5 grow, Task 6 draw); this
task only fixes the layout.

fields.name: rate_amount
docs: Tokens (draw units) added to the bucket each `period_secs`.

fields.name: period_secs
docs: Refill period in seconds.

fields.name: bucket
docs: Current bucket level (available draw velocity).

fields.name: last_refill
docs: Unix time the bucket was last refilled.

fields.name: ceiling
docs: Absolute outstanding-draw ceiling, independent of the velocity bucket.
None ⇒ velocity-only (no hard ceiling at this node).

fields.name: burst_multiple
docs: Burst cap: bucket may accumulate up to `rate_amount * burst_multiple`.

types.name: RecordCreditEventArgs
docs: Append a credit event to a human's `CreditRoot`, welding a per-agent vault to
that human. Credit history keys to the nullifier `N`, not to a vault address.

fields.name: agent_id
docs: The per-agent identifier (caller-supplied 32 bytes). Combined with the
CreditRoot's verified nullifier this MUST reproduce the vault's stored
`identity_claim` (`SHA256(nullifier ‖ agent_id)`), proving the vault
descends from this human.

fields.name: kind
docs: Opaque event payload kind (extended by the credit product).

fields.name: amount
docs: USDC amount (or product-defined unit) associated with this event.

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; challenge must be sha256(operation_message).

fields.name: session_pubkey
docs: Ed25519 pubkey the buyer's SDK generated in memory. The passkey is
endorsing this exact key — only this key can sign vouchers for the
duration of the session.

fields.name: max_amount
docs: Cumulative cap in atomic units. The seller's middleware AND any future
on-chain consumer of `active_session.spent` enforces this.

fields.name: expires_at
docs: Wall-clock expiry, unix seconds. Must be strictly in the future.

fields.name: allowed_counterparty
docs: The seller this session is bound to. Any voucher claiming a different
counterparty MUST be rejected by the seller's verification path.

fields.name: nonce
docs: Per-session nonce. Combined with `expires_at`, gives each session a
unique fingerprint for off-chain replay protection. Caller picks; the
program does not enforce monotonicity (a non-monotonic nonce is the
buyer's own footgun, not a protocol attack).

fields.name: max_revolving_capacity
docs: Cap the revolving meter (`current_outstanding`) is checked against.

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn `clientDataJSON`. Its `challenge` field must base64url-decode
to sha256(registration_message).

fields.name: authenticator_data
docs: WebAuthn `authenticatorData` (37+ bytes).

fields.name: amount
docs: USDC the caller intends to repay. Clamped to the node's outstanding
`borrowed` so an over-repay never moves more than is owed. The following
SignV2 transfers exactly the CLAMPED value (`amount.min(borrowed)`).

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn `clientDataJSON` from the browser. Its `challenge` field
must base64url-decode to `sha256(operation_message)`.

fields.name: authenticator_data
docs: WebAuthn `authenticatorData` (37+ bytes).

fields.name: allowed_counterparty
docs: The counterparty whose session PDA is being revoked. Goes in the args so
the accounts struct can re-derive the PDA seed.

types.name: RootAttached
docs: `attach_root` — an existing node was pointed UP at a human `credit_root`
(wallet-first acquire-a-root; one-human-one-root by aggregation).

types.name: RootSource
docs: How the current World ID merkle root was posted into `WorldIdRootCache`.

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; challenge must be sha256(operation_message).

fields.name: amount
docs: The slice of the defaulter's shortfall this ancestor covers. The following
SignV2 must transfer EXACTLY this amount, or the cascade is rejected.
Bounded in-handler by `<= defaulted_node.shortfall` (Model A — the only
in-handler amount bound).

types.name: SeizeCollateralArgs
docs: Empty by design. Unlike `recover_abandoned_lock` (which carries WebAuthn
ceremony bytes because the BUYER must passkey-sign their own recovery),
`seize_collateral` takes NO args: the financier is not passkey-verified in
v1, and the seized amount is computed on-chain (`min(borrowed, collateral)`)
— never a caller-supplied value. The deadline + dexter_authority are the gate.

types.name: SessionAccount
docs: A single authorized session, now a standalone account instead of a Vault
field. PDA: seeds = [SESSION_SEED, vault.key(), session.allowed_counterparty].
One per (vault, counterparty); the seed binding gives "one tab per app,
re-register replaces in place". `version == 0` means freshly-created/cleared.

fields.name: version
docs: First-touch / migration discriminator. 0 = never touched (or cleared by
revoke). SESSION_VERSION_V1 once written. NOT the Anchor discriminator
(Anchor sets that on init_if_needed before the handler runs) — this is a
program-controlled field, the only reliable new-vs-replace signal.

fields.name: bump
docs: Stored canonical bump → create_program_address (one-shot, no ~1500 CU
find_program_address search) when re-deriving siblings in the gate.

fields.name: vault
docs: The vault this session authorizes spending FROM. Bound at first touch.

fields.name: session
docs: The session registration scope — UNCHANGED struct, reused verbatim.

types.name: SessionRegistration
docs: On-chain record of an authorized session key.
The session pubkey is an ordinary ed25519 keypair the buyer's SDK generated
in memory at tab-open time. The passkey signed a 180-byte registration
message (see docs/DESIGN-vault-v2-session-keys.md §2.2) endorsing these
scope limits. From this point on, the seller's middleware accepts vouchers
signed by `session_pubkey` for this vault, up to `max_amount` and before
`expires_at`, only for `allowed_counterparty`.
`spent` is the running cumulative — incremented by settle paths that close
vouchers — so we can enforce the cap across the lifetime of the session
without an additional read.

fields.name: current_outstanding
docs: Live unsettled exposure. Rises at tab-open (settle_voucher increment),
falls at confirmed settle (settle_tab_voucher). This is the field that
REVOLVES — the credex meter.

fields.name: max_revolving_capacity
docs: Admission cap the revolving meter is checked against. Set + passkey-
endorsed at register_session_key. May be <= max_amount.

fields.name: crystallized_cumulative
docs: Session-scope monotonic locked-into-claim odometer; mirror of `spent`
for the lock terminal path. Rises at `lock_voucher`. Never decremented.
The XOR frontier `max(spent, crystallized_cumulative)` gates both
terminal paths (seam spec section 4). (Seam spec section 1.)

fields.name: last_locked_sequence
docs: Last voucher sequence number that was locked. Reserved for future
out-of-order lock detection — NOT the XOR guard (the frontier is).

fields.name: frozen
docs: Target state: `true` to freeze the subtree, `false` to thaw it.

fields.name: paused
docs: Target state: `true` to pause, `false` to unpause.

fields.name: reason
docs: Opaque reason code recorded on a pause (ignored on unpause).

fields.name: new_reserve
docs: The new aggregate ceiling to set on the root's `RateCap`. Must be
`>= node.subtree_draw` (the lowering guard).

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn `clientDataJSON` produced by the browser. Must contain a
`challenge` field equal to base64url(sha256(operation_message)).

fields.name: authenticator_data
docs: WebAuthn `authenticatorData` produced by the authenticator (37+ bytes).

fields.name: swig_id
docs: 32-byte Swig ID (HMAC-derived client-side from identity_seed + hmac_key).

fields.name: swig_account_bump
docs: Bump for swig_account.

fields.name: swig_wallet_address_bump
docs: Bump for swig_wallet_address PDA.

fields.name: dexter_master_pubkey
docs: Becomes role-2 (Ed25519Session) authority.

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON (challenge = sha256("set_swig" || swig_address_bytes)).

fields.name: authenticator_data
docs: WebAuthn authenticatorData (37+ bytes).

fields.name: channel_id
docs: Channel id from the voucher's payload — first 32 bytes of the
44-byte message the session key signed.

fields.name: cumulative_amount
docs: Total cumulative amount this voucher authorizes. Must be > the
vault's recorded `active_session.spent` (monotonic) and <= the
session's `max_amount` cap.

fields.name: sequence_number
docs: Monotonic sequence number from the voucher payload. Stored as-is in
the signed message; not currently used for replay defense (the
`spent` monotonicity check covers replay) but reserved for future
out-of-order voucher detection.

fields.name: allowed_counterparty
docs: V6: the seller this voucher pays — equals the session PDA's
`allowed_counterparty`. Carried in the args so the accounts struct can
re-derive the session PDA seed. Not part of the signed voucher message
(the message layout is unchanged); the PDA seed binding is what ties
the settle to the correct session.

fields.name: allowed_counterparty
docs: V6: the seller the tab is opened against — equals the session PDA's
`allowed_counterparty`. Carried so the accounts struct can re-derive the
(optional) session PDA seed. Only load-bearing on the increment path; on
the close path it's ignored (no session account is passed).

types.name: SubtreeReleased
docs: `emancipate` — `emancipated_node` cut its edge from `parent` and re-rooted.

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version. MUST be the first field so byte 0 of the deserialized
account directly indicates which Vault generation this is. A program
bound to v2 rejects (`VaultError::UnsupportedVaultVersion`) anything
that isn't `VAULT_VERSION_V2`.

fields.name: cooling_off_seconds
docs: Minimum delay between `request_withdrawal` and `finalize_withdrawal`.
`u32` because negative is meaningless and 136 years of seconds is plenty.

fields.name: identity_claim
docs: Operator-defined opaque identity claim (formerly `supabase_user_id`).
The protocol does not interpret these bytes; Dexter writes a Supabase
UUID prefix, future operators may write whatever they want. Documented
in the OTS spec as "operator-defined".

fields.name: dexter_authority
docs: The session authority recorded at init — the ONLY key permitted to
mutate `pending_voucher_count` (settle_voucher / force_release).

fields.name: live_session_count
docs: V6: number of LIVE session PDAs for this vault. Incremented by
register_session_key on first-touch (new counterparty), decremented by
revoke_session_key. The register-time overcommit gate requires the caller
to pass exactly this many live siblings (minus the target if it already
exists), so the summed-cap gate cannot be gamed low. Range 0..=255.

fields.name: outstanding_locked_amount
docs: Sum of unsettled LockedClaim amounts for this vault. Rises at
`lock_voucher`, falls at `settle_locked_voucher` / `recover_abandoned_lock`.
The crystallized (buyer-irrevocable) reservation tier. Read by
`finalize_withdrawal` to reject withdrawals that would violate the
reservation. (Seam spec section 1.)

fields.name: total_crystallized_amount
docs: Lifetime monotonic locked-into-claim odometer at vault scope. Never decremented.

fields.name: total_settled_amount
docs: Lifetime monotonic settled-from-claim odometer at vault scope. Never decremented.

fields.name: node
docs: The wallet↔node link. `Pubkey::default()` at init = UNLINKED (this vault
participates in no delegation graph yet). Set by `attach_node` (Task 10)
to the `PrincipalNode` PDA whose credit authority governs this wallet's
draws. The depth-N graph replaces the retired depth-1 credit fields
(`borrowed` / `standby_backer` / `standby_cap` / `borrow_recovery_at`):
all credit state now lives on the `PrincipalNode`, not the Vault.

types.name: WorldIdRootCache
docs: Singleton cache of the current World ID merkle root on Solana.
PDA: seeds = [WORLD_ID_ROOT_SEED]. Read by establish_credit_root.

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version. First field per house style.

fields.name: bump
docs: Canonical bump for this singleton PDA.

fields.name: root
docs: The World ID v4 registry merkle root (bn254 field element, big-endian).

fields.name: block_number
docs: Source Ethereum block number the root was read at (advisory in Phase 1).

fields.name: posted_at
docs: Unix time the root was last written. Freshness is enforced in Phase 2.

fields.name: source
docs: Provenance of the root — Interim (admin-gated) or Sp1Helios (trustless).
